mission.title: Install and Configure llm-d
mission.description: llm-d is a high-performance distributed inference serving stack optimized for Kubernetes, designed to achieve state-of-the-art inference performance with modern accelerators. It is ideal for production deployments requiring efficient orchestration of large language models. Key trade-offs include complexity in setup and the need for appropriate hardware resources.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d command-line interface to interact with the llm-d platform.

```bash

curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

```

> This command downloads the CLI binary, makes it executable, and moves it to the local bin directory.
commands: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new GKE cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create llm-d-cluster --cluster-version=1.31 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> This command creates a GKE cluster named 'llm-d-cluster' with 3 nodes.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to use the new cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig to use the credentials of the newly created cluster.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the nodes in the cluster to ensure they are ready.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see all nodes in the 'Ready' state.

steps.title: Install llm-d Helm Chart
steps.description:
This step installs the llm-d Helm chart into the Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

helm install llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d --create-namespace

```

> Adds the llm-d Helm repository and updates it.
> Installs the llm-d application in the 'llm-d' namespace.
commands: helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step configures autoscaling and monitoring for the llm-d deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/autoscaler.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/llm-d/llm-d/main/deploy/monitoring.yaml

```

> Applies the autoscaler configuration.
> Applies the monitoring configuration.

uninstall.title: Uninstall llm-d
uninstall.description:
This step removes the llm-d deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

helm uninstall llm-d --namespace llm-d

gcloud container clusters delete llm-d-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> Uninstalls the llm-d Helm release.
> Deletes the GKE cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade llm-d
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data, then upgrade the llm-d installation.

```bash

kubectl cp <your-pod-name>:/path/to/data /local/backup

helm upgrade llm-d llm-d/llm-d --version 0.6.0 --namespace llm-d

kubectl get pods -n llm-d

```

> Backup your data from the pod.
> Upgrades the llm-d Helm release to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running and ready after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating load balancer'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient permissions or quota in your GCP project.

**Fix:**
Check your IAM permissions and ensure you have sufficient quota for load balancers.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'ImagePullBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image could not be pulled.

**Fix:**
Verify that the image name and tag are correct and that the image is publicly accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This occurs when a pod fails to start repeatedly, often due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' to diagnose the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: '503 Service Unavailable'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the service is not able to route traffic to any pods.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the deployment has running pods and that the service is correctly configured.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced intelligent inference scheduling and workload autoscaling features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n llm-d' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.
codeSnippets: curl -LO https://github.com/llm-d/llm-d/releases/download/v0.6.0/llm-d-linux-amd64 && chmod +x llm-d-linux-amd64 && sudo mv llm-d-linux-amd64 /usr/local/bin/llm-d
helm repo add llm-d https://llm-d.github.io/llm-d/ && helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and your GCP project is set up correctly.
